question.title: Will US existing home sales (seasonally adjusted annual rate) for March 2026 be at least 4.0 million?
question.description:
This forecasts housing-market transaction volume, a key channel for consumption, credit demand, and construction-related activity.

`{"format":"bot_tournament_question","info":{"hash_id":"0fcf1dc94baf791f","sheet_id":330.1}}`
question.resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the National Association of Realtors reports March 2026 existing home sales at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.0 million or higher in its first publication reporting March 2026, released before May 1, 2026. This question resolves to no if the first reported March 2026 SAAR is below 4.0 million. The value will be taken from NAR’s Existing-Home Sales page at https://www.nar.realtor/research-and-statistics/housing-statistics/existing-home-sales
question.fine_print: Use the first reported March 2026 SAAR (ignore later revisions). Use the headline “Existing-home sales” SAAR level. Candidate Sources: NAR press release linked from the Existing-Home Sales page (backup) https://www.nar.realtor/newsroom | FRED series EXHOSLUSM495S (for cross-check only) https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/EXHOSLUSM495S
question.short_title: Will US existing home sales (seasonally adjusted annual rate) for March 2026 be at least 4.0 million
